<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.71) — 11 imports target "web-application-core"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion of 0.29 is below the 0.4 threshold, likely because the community-detection algorithm sees the four files as loosely connected within the broader viewer graph; semantically they are tightly related around hench observability.
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.71 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold; the zone has no internal edges compared to its 11 outbound imports into web-viewer, making it a pure leaf consumer cluster — splitting it further would not reduce coupling.
[observation] [info] No test files are present in this zone; the hench agent monitor panels are untested UI components, which is a gap worth addressing as the panels grow more complex.
[pattern] [info] Asymmetric bidirectional coupling (11 outbound, 4 inbound) compared to viewer-crash-recovery (3 outbound, 2 inbound) suggests this zone has a weaker interface contract — the crash zone's near-symmetric ratio indicates a more deliberate boundary design
[relationship] [warning] hench-agent-monitor bypasses the web-shared foundation layer entirely (0 imports) and depends exclusively on web-viewer (11 imports) — any future need for shared utilities will route through the viewer abstraction rather than the designated foundation layer
[anti-pattern] [warning] hench-runs.ts (751 lines) imports ActiveTasksPanel, ConcurrencyPanel, and MemoryPanel directly by file path rather than through a zone barrel — if the panel components ever need to be reorganized, hench-runs.ts must be updated alongside them; a barrel index.ts for the panel components would decouple the page view from panel file structure
[anti-pattern] [warning] hench-runs.ts at 751 lines exceeds the threshold where a page view is likely handling concerns beyond routing and composition — at this size it likely contains polling logic, state management, and layout decisions that could be extracted into the panel components it orchestrates, reducing the page to a thin composition shell
[suggestion] [warning] Add ActiveRun (and ActiveTasksPanelProps if consumed externally) to components/index.ts so the barrel can serve as the exclusive entry point for all hench-agent-monitor exports. Without it, hench-runs.ts cannot fully migrate to barrel imports even if it wanted to.
[suggestion] [warning] Update hench-runs.ts lines 19-24 to import all six panels from '../components/index.js' rather than from individual component files — the barrel already exports all five value components, and adding ActiveRun would remove the last blocker for full barrel adoption.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Hench Agent Monitor" (hench-agent-monitor) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.71, cohesion: 0.29, coupling: 0.71) — unreliable: zone has only 4 files (minimum 5 for reliable metrics)

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.29) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.71) — 11 imports target "web-application-core"
- All four files serve a single observability concern (hench runtime visibility), so the zone has a clear domain role despite low algorithmic cohesion
- The hench-runs page view sitting alongside panel components is architecturally sound — they share the same data domain and consumer context
- High coupling to web-viewer (11 imports) is expected because these components consume shared viewer primitives; it is not a red flag on its own
- Cohesion of 0.29 is below the 0.4 threshold, likely because the community-detection algorithm sees the four files as loosely connected within the broader viewer graph; semantically they are tightly related around hench observability.
- Coupling of 0.71 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold; the zone has no internal edges compared to its 11 outbound imports into web-viewer, making it a pure leaf consumer cluster — splitting it further would not reduce coupling.
- No test files are present in this zone; the hench agent monitor panels are untested UI components, which is a gap worth addressing as the panels grow more complex.
- The zone has zero imports from web-shared — all 11 outbound imports target web-viewer, meaning the foundation layer provides no direct value to this zone; if web-shared utilities are needed they must be proxied through the much larger viewer zone
- The 11-in / 4-out bidirectional ratio with web-viewer is significantly more asymmetric than the crash recovery zone's 3-in / 2-out ratio, indicating this zone is more tightly coupled to viewer internals than the crash zone despite serving a similar observability role
- hench-agent-monitor bypasses the web-shared foundation layer entirely (0 imports) and depends exclusively on web-viewer (11 imports) — any future need for shared utilities will route through the viewer abstraction rather than the designated foundation layer
- Asymmetric bidirectional coupling (11 outbound, 4 inbound) compared to viewer-crash-recovery (3 outbound, 2 inbound) suggests this zone has a weaker interface contract — the crash zone's near-symmetric ratio indicates a more deliberate boundary design
- hench-runs.ts is 751 lines and acts as the composition root that imports and orchestrates all three panel components (ActiveTasksPanel, ConcurrencyPanel, MemoryPanel) plus six additional viewer modules — at this size the page view has grown beyond pure routing/composition and likely contains layout and state-management logic that belongs in the individual panels
- hench-runs.ts imports directly from individual panel component files (active-tasks-panel.js, concurrency-panel.js, memory-panel.js) rather than through a zone barrel — this means the page view is coupled to the internal file structure of its sibling panels, not just their public interfaces
- The zone groups a 751-line page view (hench-runs.ts) with three panel components that it directly orchestrates — the page is the only consumer of all three panels, making the zone a composition unit rather than a cohesion unit; the low cohesion score (0.29) reflects this architectural reality
- hench-runs.ts (751 lines) imports ActiveTasksPanel, ConcurrencyPanel, and MemoryPanel directly by file path rather than through a zone barrel — if the panel components ever need to be reorganized, hench-runs.ts must be updated alongside them; a barrel index.ts for the panel components would decouple the page view from panel file structure
- hench-runs.ts at 751 lines exceeds the threshold where a page view is likely handling concerns beyond routing and composition — at this size it likely contains polling logic, state management, and layout decisions that could be extracted into the panel components it orchestrates, reducing the page to a thin composition shell
- A components/index.ts barrel already exists and exports all five panel components (ActiveTasksPanel, ConcurrencyPanel, MemoryPanel, WsHealthPanel, ThrottleControlsPanel), yet hench-runs.ts bypasses it on every import — importing each of the six panels directly by individual file path (lines 19-24). This is an active bypass of an existing barrel, not a case of a missing barrel as previously implied.
- The ActiveRun interface (active-tasks-panel.ts line 24) is exported from the component file but absent from components/index.ts — its omission from the barrel forces hench-runs.ts to import it directly even if the page view otherwise switched to barrel imports, making the barrel incomplete as a single-entry-point for the zone's public API.
- hench-runs.ts bypasses the components barrel for both value imports (ActiveTasksPanel, ConcurrencyPanel, MemoryPanel, WsHealthPanel, ThrottleControlsPanel) and a type import (ActiveRun) — the bypass is total, not partial, meaning the barrel has zero practical consumers among the panels' direct orchestrator.
- Add ActiveRun (and ActiveTasksPanelProps if consumed externally) to components/index.ts so the barrel can serve as the exclusive entry point for all hench-agent-monitor exports. Without it, hench-runs.ts cannot fully migrate to barrel imports even if it wanted to.
- Update hench-runs.ts lines 19-24 to import all six panels from '../components/index.js' rather than from individual component files — the barrel already exports all five value components, and adding ActiveRun would remove the last blocker for full barrel adoption.
- [call graph] 25 internal calls, 1 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 0.96, coupling: 0.04)

</insights>
